structure: Level 0 abstract. Domain details in .claude/stack/{domain}.json — load only when working in that domain.
philosophy: Use as little AI as possible — combine deterministic logic with LLMs.
Ship small, iterate fast. Proof of concept before polish.
Every layer should work independently before integrating with the next.
Tie pricing and value to measurable outcomes, not feature counts.
If you turn the system off and nobody calls within an hour, it's not solving a real problem.
Code is a commodity — protect the system's structural integrity, not the craft of writing it.
Manual coding is the primary source of entropy — AI-generated code with architectural governance is more stable than hand-written code without it.
Natural language is the highest abstraction layer of programming — the quality of output is proportional to the structural precision of input.
Treat AI-generated code as technical debt until simplified — AI favors bloat because it fulfills probabilistic patterns.
Build from first principles — avoid unverified frameworks and black-box agent harnesses. Build a foundation you understand, own, and can debug.

engineering_methodology.description: The engineer's role has shifted from implementation laborer to system governor. Success is measured by architectural intent precision and governance rigor, not syntax proficiency.
core_pillars: 1. component-thinking: govern at the building-block level, not the line level — define interconnections, data flows, and failure modes
2. full-stack-oversight: direct the entire stack to eliminate human bottlenecks — if a project stalls because a specialist is unavailable, the system velocity is wasted
3. communication-clarity: treat natural language as high-precision programming syntax — vague instructions yield broken code
4. ruthless-simplification: strip AI-generated bloat to first principles — a codebase that is simple is a codebase that is understood
5. systematic-testing: maintain ultimate authority over reliability by rejecting AI optimism — the LLM will write passing tests that fail in production

critical_areas: data-flow-orchestration: design precise pathways for how data moves between backend, storage, and UI
component-wiring: define how modular blocks interact — interfaces between services must be robust and typed
failure-mode-analysis: anticipate cascading failures — architect graceful degradation, not collapse
component_design.constraint_based_research: Before generating code, ground AI agents in external truth — feed specific documentation, architectural references, and established patterns into context.

quality_checklist: contextual-grounding: provide the WHY behind this component to align AI logic with your intent
architectural-intent: define data schema, specific libraries, and failure recovery paths
constraint-definition: explicitly forbid known anti-patterns and unnecessary dependencies
ground-truth: supply relevant documentation to constrain generation to current best practices

simplification_loop: 2. build-implement: use AI to generate foundational components
3. test-validate: confirm implementation meets the functional requirement
4. question-research: force AI to justify naming conventions, file structures, every decision made during build
5. ruthless-simplify: remove every line and component that does not break the test suite

parallel_agency.requirement: A change in the database schema must be instantly reflected in the API contract and frontend state management.
parallel_agency.anti_pattern: Hyper-specialization creates bottlenecks — the governor must be comfortable directing across the entire lifecycle.

context_management: context-priming: point AI to 2-3 specific files and relevant documentation — prevents hallucinations and saves context window
plan-then-execute: verify AI's logic in plan mode before allowing code generation
skills-folder: maintain reusable AI skill definitions as Markdown files — repeatable tasks become standardized prompts
governor_checklist: naming-conventions: does AI-generated code match the project's established patterns?
folder-structure: are files in the correct directories per the standard template?
architectural-integrity: has the AI introduced non-standard libraries or clever logic that breaks the pattern?
dependency-audit: has the AI added unnecessary dependencies that increase the attack surface or maintenance burden?

patterns: unit-tests: service layer logic, utility functions, validators
integration-tests: API endpoints with real database (not mocks)
llm-tests: deterministic inputs with expected output patterns, LLM-as-judge for quality
rag-evaluation: retrieval precision/recall on annotated dataset
e2e-tests: critical user flows through the full stack
principles: Integration tests hit a real database — mocks hide real bugs.
LLM outputs are tested for structure and constraints, not exact text.
Every bug fix comes with a regression test.
Tests run in Docker to match production environment.
CI blocks merge on test failure — no exceptions.

mandates: reject-mocking-trap: actively reject tests that mock away the logic being validated
edge-case-authority: the human identifies outliers AI misses — network latency, race conditions, malformed inputs
framework-mastery: understand pytest/vitest well enough to audit AI-generated test logic
automated-governance: no code enters main without systematic automated validation
ai_optimism_defense.question: Ask 'What could really go wrong here?' — AI handles the happy path, the human architect identifies the outliers.

backend.stack: Python 3.12+, FastAPI, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL

frontend.stack: TypeScript, React 18+, Vite, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Zustand

ai.stack: LangChain, LangGraph, Langfuse, hybrid RAG (BM25 + vectors + RRF + cross-encoder), pgvector/numpy

infrastructure.stack: Docker, Docker Compose, Nginx, GitHub Actions, VPS, Redis, Celery Beat, Grafana

security.stack: JWT + refresh tokens, bcrypt, rate limiting, CORS whitelist, webhook signature verification

product.stack: Discovery protocol, sprint model, scoping discipline, accuracy paradigm, delivery methodology
